task: Summarize the following function in 2-4 sentences.
Describe what this function does, its inputs, and its outputs.
Write a concise natural-language description of the function below.
Explain the purpose and behavior of the function in plain prose.
Generate a documentation summary for the function provided.
Read the function and produce a brief summary of its behavior.
Write a docstring-style summary describing the function's role.
Provide a 1-3 sentence overview of what this function accomplishes.
Summarize what the function does, including any notable edge cases.
Describe the algorithmic intent of the function in human language.
Explain the function's behavior at the level a maintainer would need.
guard: Do not include implementation details. Be concise.
Avoid restating the code line by line.
Do not speculate beyond what the code shows.
Stay strictly within the scope of the given function.
Do not add usage examples or describe call sites.
Avoid jargon when plain language suffices.
Do not include code in your response.
Be precise and avoid filler phrases.
Avoid markdown formatting and code fences in your output.
Do not include questions or hedging language.
Do not invent behavior the code does not exhibit.
format: Respond in plain prose. No bullet points.
Output a single paragraph of natural language.
Output as a single sentence summary.
Use plain text only - no headers, lists, or code blocks.
Respond with prose written in the present tense.
Output a brief paragraph with no preamble.
Format as a docstring-style description, omitting triple quotes.
Use third-person and present tense throughout.
Begin directly with the description; omit phrases like 'This function'.
Respond with one tight paragraph; do not introduce yourself or restate the task.
